business.headline: Netflix shares slide over 9% as co-founder Reed Hastings steps downHastings has been a key figure in shaping Netflix into what it is today. He co-founded the company and played a major role in transforming it from a DVD-by-mail service into a global streaming giant.

Forced to mis-sell: IIIT graduate quits Rs 17 LPA bank job, cites sales pressureChirag Madaan's resignation from his Rs 17 lakh per annum banking job has reignited focus on mis-selling at financial institutions, an issue IndiaToday.in had earlier highlighted through its investigation into how sales targets and incentives shape what customers are sold.

Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani to become Asia's richestThe latest ranking places Gautam Adani at the top spot in Asia, with Mukesh Ambani now in second place. Both remain among the top 25 richest people globally, even as their wealth levels have seen changes this year.

2% to 4% DA hike likely: Here's how much your monthly pay may riseA fresh DA revision is around the corner, and early estimates suggest a 2% to 4% hike, but how much extra could this actually add to your monthly salary? Let's have a look.

India slips to 6th-largest economy: What IMF's latest data showsIndia has slipped out of the world's top five economies in nominal GDP terms, going by the IMF's latest World Economic Outlook estimates. The change says more about currency than growth, with projections suggesting the loss in ranking may not last long.

Markets open flat as geopolitical tensions ease, but caution remainsThe BSE Sensex was trading at 78,030.99, up 42.31 points or 0.05% at around 9:43 am. The Nifty 50, however, slipped marginally by 6.85 points or 0.03% to 24,189.90.

Gurugram circle rates increase by up to 75%: How much will home prices rise?The revision in Gurugram circle rates comes at a time when the city continues to see strong demand due to its proximity to the airport and the presence of major offices and industries.

Rs 8 lakh crore stuck: How delayed payments are hurting India's MSMEsAcross India, the total value of such pending payments to micro, small and medium enterprises is estimated at Rs 8.1 lakh crore, according to industry estimates and government assessments.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock markets rise or fall today?GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 24,178 at 8:06 am, suggesting that the Nifty 50 may open close to its previous session's level of 24,196.75. This points to a muted start for the markets after recent volatility.

Gold is rising slowly: Should you expect a big move soon?Gold has been moving up, but quietly. There's no sudden surge, just a gradual rise. So the big question is—does this signal a strong rally ahead or more sideways movement?

IMF warns Asia may face sharper hit from Middle East energy shockAsia relies heavily on imported oil and gas, particularly from the Middle East. This makes it more vulnerable when supply disruptions or price spikes occur.

Gold, equities or debt? Expert says no asset stays on top for longIf you're waiting to invest in the "best" asset, you may be chasing a moving target. An expert explains why gold, equities and debt keep rotating as top performers over time.

NPS, EPF or mutual funds: Where should you invest Rs 5,000 for 10 yearsYou set aside Rs 5,000 every month with one simple goal, i.e., to build something meaningful over 10 years. But the real question is where to invest it. NPS, EPF or mutual funds? Let's have a closer look.

Sensex slips 123 points, Nifty ends below 24,200 as markets erase early gainsThe BSE Sensex closed at 77,988.68, down 122.56 points or 0.16%. The NSE Nifty 50 settled at 24,196.75, lower by 34.55 points or 0.14%.

Sensex falls 1,000 points after early gains: What should investors do?The BSE Sensex moved within a wide range of 1,055.39 points, touching a high of 78,730.32 and a low of 77,674.93. The NSE Nifty 50 also saw similar movement, trading in a range of 298.15 points between a high of 24,400.95 and a low of 24,102.80.

Income below exemption limit? These 3 transactions make ITR filing compulsoryNo taxable income doesn't always mean no ITR. Certain transactions can bring you under the tax net, even if your earnings are below the basic limit.

From Rs 50 to Rs 200: How you can save up to Rs 1.05 lakh with meal vouchersWhat if your daily lunch could help you save tax? With the limit rising from Rs 50 to Rs 200 per meal, meal vouchers are suddenly becoming a powerful tax-saving tool for salaried employees.

DA hike has been delayed, but will you get it this year?If you've been waiting for the DA hike announcement, you might have noticed the silence this time. It hasn't come when expected, but does that mean trouble, or just a delay?

Workforce shrinks, pay hikes normalise: How TCS is reshaping hiring amid AI boomTCS has restored its salary cycle, but with more selective hiring and differentiated pay, signalling a shift towards skills and performance as the key drivers of workforce growth.

Mark Mobius dies at 89: The investor who took global money to emerging marketsMark Mobius built his reputation by investing in emerging markets that many investors once avoided due to political risk, weak governance and currency volatility. His approach helped draw billions of dollars into these regions.

entertainment.headline: Didn't eat a mountain: Patralekhaa blasts paps for body-shaming in viral video Actor Patralekhaa has strongly reacted to a viral video by a paps account that commented on her postpartum body. Calling out the paparazzi for insensitivity, she shared a note urging more understanding of how women's bodies change after childbirth.

Bhooth Bangla box office Day 1 prediction: Akshay's film eyes Jolly LLB 3-like start Bhooth Bangla is eyeing a respectable opening at the box office despite tempered pre-release expectations. With advance bookings and paid previews indicating steady interest, the horror-comedy could land close to the opening numbers of Jolly LLB 3 if momentum holds.

Alia Bhatt's sister Shaheen engaged to fitness instructor Ishaan Mehra. Pics Alia Bhatt's sister Shaheen got engaged to fitness trainer Ishaan Mehra. The couple shared the update on social media with a series of pictures.

Kangana Ranaut reacts to Chirag Paswan dating buzz with a witty remark Actor-politician Kangana Ranaut has set the record straight on her bond with her fellow parliamentarian Chirag Paswan. She clarified that Chirag is her friend and there is no romantic relationship between them.

Bhooth Bangla X reviews: Akshay Kumar-Priyadarshan's comedy gets mixed response Akshay Kumar-Priyadarshan's horror comedy, Bhooth Bangla received a mixed response in its early reviews. The film's paid previews were held on April 16.

Couldn't protect Jana Nayagan, claims to protect Tamil Nadu: DMK MP jabs Vijay The leak of Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan has stirred political controversy with Rajya Sabha MP, P Wilson, questioning the actor's political competence. Meanwhile, the Tamil Nadu Cyber Crime Wing has arrested nine suspects and is investigating the breach.

Katy Perry crashes prom in viral video from night of Ruby Rose's claims. Watch The sexual assault allegations made by actor Ruby Rose against singer Katy Perry have sparked widespread attention. A video from the same night is now going viral. Perry has denied the claims by calling them false and harmful.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 29: Only Rs 5 crore away from dethroning Pushpa 2 Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is inching closer to a major global box office milestone, with its latest numbers putting it within striking distance of Pushpa 2: The Rule. Check the latest worldwide collection update inside.

The film struggles with outdated humour, weak storytelling, and lacks the excitement audiences hoped for.

Matka King review: Vijay Varma wins big, but the series misses the jackpotRelease Date:Fri Apr 17, 2026 Matka King is a gripping tale of gambling and integrity in the 1970s Bombay. Directed by Nagraj Manjule, the series is led by actor Vijay Varma.

Bhooth Bangla box office: Decent paid preview numbers to boost Akshay Kumar film A strong preview turnout boosts Bhooth BanBhooth Bangla has opened stronger than expected with impressive paid preview numbers, surpassing early trade projections. Check out the detailed report inside.

Siddharth Jadhav on Rohit Shetty's Simmba: I started getting more work Actor Siddharth Jadhav reflected on his unplanned journey from Marathi theatre to films. He said Simmba marked a turning point and brought more work opportunities.

Avengers: Doomsday trailer: Thor attacks Stormbreaker, Doom blocks him Marvel Studios revealed the first trailer for Avengers: Doomsday at CinemaCon 2026, generating huge excitement among fans. The film promises intense battles, a multiverse storyline, and the return of beloved characters along with new recruits.

I try not to judge my characters: Kritika Kamra returns in crime drama Matka King Kritika Kamra prefers not to be boxed in "good girl" and "bad girl" roles, and says it doesn't matter if her characters are likeable to all. The actor returns in the crime drama series Matka King.

No one better to play Lord Rama: Ramayana producer's praise for Ranbir Kapoor Ramayana producer Namit Malhotra heaped praise on Ranbir Kapoor, calling him the finest actor of our generation. He also backed his casting as Lord Rama in Ramayana.

Ramayana, Adipurush, Mahadev: Our screen gods look straight out of the gym lately From films to soaps, Indian pop culture is increasingly portraying male gods from Ramayana, Mahabharata and Puranas as gym-toned hunks who could give Chris Hemsworth's Thor a run for his money.

RGV: Dhurandhar 2 shows Dawood Ibrahim incorrectly, he is inactive by choice Director Ram Gopal Varma questioned the portrayal of gangster Dawood Ibrahim in Dhurandhar 2. While praising the film, he suggested Dawood may have stepped away from crime years ago.

Babil Khan shares how Irrfan cared for wife during cancer, posts throwback pics Actor Babil Khan shared a heartfelt memory of late father Irrfan caring for his wife during his cancer battle. The celebrated actor died in 2020 at 53.

Gaurav Gera: Aditya Dhar handles pressure with ease, has a no-chaos work style Gaurav Gera praised Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar's calm and composed nature while working on the blockbuster film. He shared how Dhar's evolved mindset and easygoing approach on set left a lasting impression on him.

general news.headline: BJP laid 'anti-women' trap. Opposition was ready with caste plankThe BJP sought to position itself as a champion of women's empowerment as it pushed through the women's reservation and delimitation bills. However, the opposition was equally shrewd. It raised the caste census bogey to avoid the BJP's trap of being labelled anti-women if the bills fail to clear the Lok Sabha.

Twist in conman Sukesh's case, actor Jacqueline Fernandez seeks to turn approverUpon hearing the request, the court informed Jacqueline Fernandez that she would have to make a formal request to the Enforcement Directorate (ED) for the same.

Am I a criminal? Pawan Khera asks as SC pauses bail in case by Assam CM's wifeThe Supreme Court paused Pawan Khera's interim bail in a defamation case, refused to extend his protection till Tuesday, and directed him to seek anticipatory bail before a competent Assam court.

Govt goes from fast-tracking women's quota to saving the very law, all in one dayA late-night Law Ministry notification, enforcing the 2023 Act on women's reservation, has sparked questions about its timing considering it comes as Parliament debates a contentious amendment to that very Act.

How a wrong turn gave Bombay, not capital Calcutta, India's first passenger trainThe Indian Railways network, which marked its 173rd anniversary on Thursday, started with a 33-km Bombay-Thane line. Calcutta, the British Raj's capital, would have got India's first passenger train, but everything changed after a wrong turn by a ship and a shipwreck.

Women's quota ready, but held hostage: Tharoor slams linkage to delimitationCongress MP Shashi Tharoor criticised the government for linking women's reservation to delimitation, calling it a move that holds the aspirations of women "hostage" and delays an idea that already has broad political consensus.

TCS Nashik case accused Nida Khan claims she is pregnant, seeks court reliefEight people have been arrested so far in the BPO harassment and conversion case, while one of the accused, Nida Khan, remained on the run. Her lawyer now says she is in Mumbai and has moved court for anticipatory bail, with the plea likely to be heard in Nashik.

Calm, witty and razor-sharp - Priyanka Gandhi pulled no punches in ParliamentWithout resorting to bitterness or name-calling, Priyanka Gandhi, while speaking on delimitation, delivered barbs with a smile -- drawing laughter from Home Minister Amit Shah, energising Opposition benches, and earning praise from Rahul Gandhi.

Live: Amit Shah holds key meet, Lok Sabha to vote on bills at 4 pmSpecial Parliament Session Live Updates: The Lok Sabha witnessed chaotic scenes on Friday as members of the Opposition and Treasury benches clashed during discussions on the Women's Reservation and Delimitation Bills. The House erupted in protests and counter-arguments, disrupting proceedings as debate on the contentious legislation continued. All DMK MPs wore black in protest against the notification of the Women's Reservation Bill. Meanwhile, Congress MP Rahul Gandhi is scheduled to address the Lok Sabha at 3 pm today. In Rajya Sabha, Harivansh Narayan Singh was elected Deputy Chairman of the Upper house following a formal selection in the Upper House. Union Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal is expected to move the passage of the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Bill, 2026 and the Delimitation Bill, 2026 in the Lok Sabha later in the day. Follow indiatoday.in for live updates.

Serious matter, no one will be shielded: Fadnavis on TCS Nashik sex abuse caseDevendra Fadnavis said the Nashik BPO case is very serious and assured strict action, stating no one will be shielded. He added that central agencies have been informed and will be involved in the probe, which suggests a larger module with wider reach that authorities plan to fully investigate.

People paid Rs 8 crore to run, lift at Hyrox Bengaluru event that has India talkingAround 9,000 people gathered at the Bangalore International Exhibition Centre on the weekend to do workouts one could easily do in a park or gym. Each person paid Hyrox over Rs 9,000 for that. It has sparked a debate over whether Hyrox workouts are merely a status symbol or a shift in how India approaches fitness.

sports.headline: Varun Chakravarthy to emerge stronger: Kartik Tyagi ahead of KKR against GT IPL 2026: Kartik Tyagi has backed Varun Chakravarthy to overcome his poor start to the IPL season, saying tough phases help players grow. With KKR struggling early, the team will hope Chakravarthy regains form against Gujarat Titans to revive their campaign.

Messi v Ronaldo as club owners? Argentine star acquires Catalan club UE Cornella Lionel Messi has acquired 100 per cent of UE Cornella, a Barcelona-based club known for producing stars like Andre Onana and Jordi Alba, mirroring Cristiano Ronaldo's recent stake in UD Almeria.

Wealth of knowledge: How Hayden is guiding GT's Glenn Phillips in IPL 2026 Glenn Phillips is picking the brain of legendary coach Matthew Hayden to master the mental game and find his process zone ahead of the Gujarat Titans' high-stakes IPL 2026 clash against KKR.

How Arshdeep snapped back to form vs MI: Ponting reveals chat behind the scenes IPL 2026: Arshdeep Singh's return to form came down to clarity, not just skill. A timely chat with Ricky Ponting helped him reset and deliver when PBKS needed him most.

More misery for KKR? Rahane's side set to face spin test vs Gujarat Titans IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders travel to Ahmedabad to play Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans. It will not be easy for Ajinkya Rahane's side who face in-form Rashid Khan and Prasidh Krishna.

Fatigue post-T20 World Cup? Ashwin flags concern for Mumbai Indians' IPL season R Ashwin warns that T20 World Cup fatigue is hitting Mumbai Indians' stars after a 4th IPL 2026 loss. Hardik Pandya hints at "tough calls" as MI struggles to find answers with the ball.

Kolkata disaster: Is Rahane only one to be blamed for KKR's IPL 2026 flopshow KKR are in turmoil and the blame currently seems to be firmly on Ajinkya Rahane and his methods. But is the KKR skipper just the only reason that they find themselves at the bottom of the IPL points table?

No pressure on Bumrah: Coach Jayawardene says MI bowling lacking collectively IPL 2026: MI head coach Mahela Jayawardene has backed Jasprit Bumrah despite his wicketless run this season. However, he admitted Mumbai Indians' bowling struggles are a collective issue, not an individual one.

Grinning ear to ear: Preity Zinta relishing PBKS's dominant win over MI IPL 2026: Preity Zinta couldn't hide her joy after PBKS' dominant win over MI, saying she was "grinning ear to ear." The co-owner celebrated the performance with a heartfelt post as Punjab Kings continued their unbeaten run.

Kohli likes another Instagram influencer's photo, fans flood comments section IPL 2026: A simple Instagram like by Virat Kohli sparked a viral "algorithm" meme fest online. Fans quickly revived his old explanation, turning yet another social media moment into light-hearted humour.

All-round Arshdeep Singh: Viral reels spiking Punjab Kings' fanbase, says pacer Arshdeep Singh took some credit for the spike in Punjab Kings' fan base, saying that his social media game is one of the reasons behind the increase in followers for the franchise. Arshdeep picked up three wickets in the win against MI.

IPL Play of the Day: Ice-cold Shreyas Iyer proves he is the new King of ChasesMI vs PBKS: Shreyas Iyer downed Mumbai Indians with a terrific innings of 66 off 35 balls. This was the third straight match where Shreyas hit a fifty in the chase, helping the team remain unbeaten this season.

Vinod Kambli is fine: Wife addresses health speculationFormer cricketer Vinod Kambli's wife, Andrea Hewitt, has dismissed speculation around his health, stating that the former India batter is 'fine'. Her statement comes amid reports suggesting Kambli was battling serious medical issues and remained at risk of a brain stroke.

Frustrated Hardik Pandya slams teammates after embarrassing loss vs Punjab KingsMI vs PBKS: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya cut a frustrated figure after the team's loss against Punjab Kings at the Wankhede Stadium. Asked what went wrong in the game, Hardik said they were outplayed by the visiting team on Thursday, April 16.

Prabhsimran-powered Punjab stay unbeaten, hand Mumbai a reality checkPrabhsimran's 80 and a captain's knock from chase master Shreyas Iyer was enough for Punjab Kings to remain unbeaten in the IPL 2026 campaign and pile on the misery on Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium.

Rs 1cr bargain: Quinton de Kock hits 53-ball ton in timely reminder to MI coachesIPL 2026, MI vs PBKS: Getting his first chance of the season after an injury to Rohit Sharma, Quinton de Kock proved he is not suited for warming the benches. The veteran wicketkeeper-batter smashed 112 off 60 balls, making an immediate impact for the Mumbai Indians.

Manchester City captain Bernardo Silva to leave the club at end of seasonManchester City captain Bernardo Silva will depart the club at the end of the season after the expiry of his contract. Bernardo has won 19 major honours with City, including a Champions League title.

Shreyas Iyer defies gravity for relay catch in Mumbai, Rohit's reaction goes viralShreyas Iyer stunned the Mumbai crowd on Thursday, April 16, pulling off a ridiculous save at the boundary line. Shreyas' gravity defying effort resulted in Hardik Pandya's dismissal in Mumbai Indians vs Punjab Kings.

MS Dhoni sparks frenzy in Hyderabad, fans throng airport for a glimpse. VideoMS Dhoni received a rousing welcome in Hyderabad as he travelled for his first away game of IPL 2026. The Chennai Super Kings star's wife shared videos of fan frenzy at the Hyderabad airport on Instagram on Thursday.

Jay Shah named in prestigious Young Global Leaders by World Economic ForumJay Shah has been named in the Young Global Leaders list by the World Economic Forum. The forum recognised Shah for his growing influence in global cricket and his role in expanding the sport through the International Cricket Council.

MI vs PBKS: Suryakumar fails again as India captaincy rumours continue to swirlMI vs PBKS, IPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav was dismissed for a golden duck by Arshdeep Singh in Mumbai on Thursday. The T20 World Cup-winning captain has struggled for consistency in the new season of the IPL.

Huge blow for MI: Injured Rohit sidelined for more than one game in IPL 2026Hardik Pandya has revealed that Rohit Sharma will be out for at least a couple of games as he failed to recover in time for the clash against PBKS.

FIFA hits back at USA for asking world body to subsidise travel costs for fansFIFA has hit back at the United States after local authorities asked the governing body to subsidise fan travel costs for the FIFA World Cup 2026. Tension has been rising over reports that matchday transport could cost close to $100.

In IPL exile, Lalit Modi places his bet on oldest sport in the worldIPL founder Lalit Modi is working on his next big idea, the former cricket administrator revealed in a chat with former England captain Michael Vaughan. Modi explained his vision of creating a new sporting league, which he believed would find guaranteed success all over the world.

R Ashwin backs ex-RCB pacer to be CSK's replacement for Khaleel AhmedR Ashwin has endorsed former RCB pacer Umesh Yadav as CSK's replacement for Khaleel Ahmed in the ongoing IPL 2026 season. Khaleel was injured during the KKR win and will miss the remainder of the IPL 2026 campaign.

MI vs PBKS Highlights: Punjab Kings thrash Mumbai Indians by 7 wicketsMumbai (MI) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: PBKS (198/3 in 16.3 ovs). Shreyas Iyer and Prabhsimran Singh have guided Punjab Kings to a terrific victory over Mumbai Indians at the Wankhede Stadium. With the win, Punjab remain unbeaten in the tournament.

After Cummins return news, SRH dealt new injury blow as pacer ruled of IPL 2026Sunrisers Hyderabad have been dealt a new injury blow as overseas pacer David Payne has been ruled out of the remainder of the IPL 2026 season. Hyderabad are set to welcome back Pat Cummins into the mix after his back issue was resolved.

Lalit Modi explains why IND vs PAK bilaterals may never happenLalit Modi says political tensions and a "politicised" PCB are key reasons behind the absence of India vs Pakistan bilaterals, adding he does not see a revival happening anytime soon.

MS Dhoni back? CSK great sets off to Hyderabad, raises comeback hopesIPL 2026: After opting not to travel to Guwahati and Bengaluru for their previous away games, MS Dhoni joined the Chennai Super KIngs squad which left for Hyderabad on Thursday. CSK will play SunRisers Hyderabad on Saturday, April 18.

RCB's golden era? Bangar predicts title spree for Royal Challengers BengaluruSanjay Bangar sees RCB entering a golden era after their maiden IPL title, backing the in-form side to build a winning culture and challenge for multiple championships in the coming years.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
